executive_summary: The Iran-US conflict has escalated dramatically with Iran's Supreme Leader confirmed killed in a US-Israeli coordinated strike. Over 1,000 IRGC members and 555 Iranian civilians are confirmed dead. US forces have destroyed all Iranian warships in Oman and struck the Natanz nuclear facility. Gulf states report their Patriot air defenses will be depleted within 4 days. The Strait of Hormuz is now effectively closed as insurers cancel coverage and IRGC threatens to attack any transiting ship. US casualties have risen to 6 troops.
whats_new: Trump declares 'I got him before he got me' after Iran's Supreme Leader killed in Israeli strike on Tehran - confirmed by multiple sources
UAE Defense Ministry: Air Defense Forces Destroyed Over 160 Ballistic Missiles from Iran - largest single interception claim
Qatar and UAE warn Patriot air defense systems will be depleted within 4 days, request urgent international assistance
US Central Command confirms death toll rises to 6 American troops, with remains of 2 additional soldiers recovered
Iran claims US-Israeli attack on Natanz nuclear facility - represents escalation to critical nuclear infrastructure
Qatar sends formal diplomatic message to UN Security Council regarding Iranian aggression on its territory
Bahrain's permanent mission on behalf of GCC states sends urgent letter to UN Secretary-General about Iranian missile attacks
Israel issues evacuation orders for 31 villages in southern Lebanon as operations expand against Hezbollah
confirmed_events: Gulf states (Bahrain, Qatar, UAE) intercepting hundreds of Iranian missiles and drones - Bahrain 70 missiles/59 drones, Qatar destroyed 2 Iranian Su-24 jets and 7 ballistic missiles, UAE intercepted 9 ballistic missiles/6 cruise missiles/148 drones
US forces destroyed all 11 Iranian warships in Gulf of Oman and 11 drone carrier vessels - major elimination of Iranian naval capability
Israeli Air Force completed three additional attack waves in western Iran targeting missile launchers and defense systems
Israeli forces struck broadcasting authority compound in Tehran's Ain neighborhood - escalation inside Iranian capital
IRGC launched Wave 12 of 'Honest Promise 4' operations targeting US and Israeli sites with 700 drones and hundreds of missiles
unverified_emerging: IRGC official threatens to 'set ablaze' any ships in Strait of Hormuz - single source (Middle East Eye) but represents major escalation in energy warfare
Photo published of graves of over 160 girls killed in Minab school attack - claim from Iraqji source, unverified
Explosions reported in Jordan near American base - single source (Mivzakim Breaking), unconfirmed
Iran claims drone strike on US Marine base in Kuwait - IRGC press service claim, not independently verified
Trump claims Iran could have obtained nuclear weapons three years ago if US stayed in JCPOA - political statement, unverifiable

people_travel: Airports closures and flight disruptions across region - multiple airports affected
Mass evacuation orders issued for 31 villages in southern Lebanon - civilian displacement ongoing
Dubai no longer considered safe haven - missile attacks directly targeting city
supply_chain: Strait of Hormuz effectively closed - world's largest insurers cancel war-risk coverage for Persian Gulf vessels
Energy prices rising globally - 20% of world oil supplies at risk from potential Hormuz closure
Supply chain disruption from port closures and shipping route blocks in Gulf region
market_macro: Global energy prices spiking due to Hormuz closure threat
International stock markets declining on regional escalation
Major economic implications from potential disruption to 20% of world oil transit

outlook_24_72h.base_case: Conflict will intensify with Iran leadership succession crisis and continued IRGC missile/drone campaigns against US bases and Gulf allies. Gulf states face critical air defense depletion within 4 days without international replenishment. Strait of Hormuz closure threat poses immediate global energy supply risk. Israel expanding operations into Lebanon against Hezbollah while US maintains pressure on Iranian nuclear facilities. US casualties expected to rise as Iranian retaliation continues.
escalation_risks: Iran executes Strait of Hormuz threat - attacks on commercial shipping would trigger global energy crisis and likely US escalation
Nuclear facility strikes (Natanz) could trigger Iranian response with asymmetric attacks or WMD escalation
Gulf state air defense depletion could lead to strategic defeats and regional instability
Hezbollah expansion of attacks on Israel could draw US deeper into multi-front conflict
Iranian cyberattacks on critical infrastructure in US or allied nations
outlook_24_72h.de_escalation_pathways: De-escalation appears unlikely in near-term as both sides have committed to maximalist objectives. Iran faces leadership succession crisis but IRGC appears determined to continue resistance. US has not defined clear endgame. International pressure from UN could potentially create diplomatic off-ramp if Gulf states mediate, but current trajectory suggests continued escalation for at least 7-14 days before possible exhaustion or negotiated ceasefire.
